author: John Chrysostom
book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.

ccel.title: NPNF1-10. St. Chrysostom: Homilies on the Gospel of Saint Matthew

source.title: NPNF1-10. St. Chrysostom: Homilies on the Gospel of Saint Matthew

title: Matthew XIV. 13.

source.title: NPNF1-10. St. Chrysostom: Homilies on the Gospel of Saint Matthew

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “But when Jesus heard of it, He departed thence by ship into a desert place apart; and when the multitudes had heard thereof, they followed Him on foot out of all the cities.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: See Him on every occasion “departing,” For not by His appearance only, but by His actions He would have this confirmed, because He knew the devil’s craft, and that he would leave nothing undone to destroy this doctrine.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: He then for this end retires; but the multitudes not even so withdraw themselves from Him, but they follow, riveted to Him, and not even John’s tragical end alarmed them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: So great a thing is earnest desire, so great a thing is love; in such wise doth it overcome and dispel all dangers.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Therefore they straightway also received their reward.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For “Jesus,” it is said, “went forth, and saw a great multitude, and was moved with compassion toward them, and He healed their sick.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For great as their assiduity was, yet nevertheless His doings exceeded what any diligence could earn.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore He sets forth also His motive for so healing them, His mercy, intense mercy: and He healeth all.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And He requires not faith here.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For both by coming to Him, and by leaving their cities, and by diligently seeking Him, and by abiding with Him even when hunger was pressing, they display their own faith.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But He is about to feed them also.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And He doth not this of Himself, but waits to be entreated; on every occasion, as I have said, maintaining this rule, not to spring onward to His miracles, preventing them, but upon some call.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And why did none of the multitude come near and speak for them?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: They reverenced Him exceedingly, and felt not even their hunger, through their longing to stay with Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Neither indeed do His disciples, when they were come to Him, say, “Feed them;” for as yet they were rather in an imperfect state; but what?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “And when it was evening,” it is said, “His disciples came to Him, saying, This is a desert place, and the time is now passed; send the multitude away, that they may go and buy themselves victuals.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For if even after the miracle they forgot what had been done, and after the baskets, supposed Him to be speaking of loaves, when He gave the name of “leaven” to the doctrine of the Pharisees;much less, when they had never yet had experience of such a miracle, would they have expected any such thing.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And yet He had made a beginning by actually healing many sick; but nevertheless, not even from this did they expect the miracle of the loaves; so imperfect were they as yet.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But mark thou, I pray, the Teacher’s skill, how distinctly He summons them on towards believing.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For He said not at once, “I feed them;” which indeed would not have been easily received; but what?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “But Jesus,” so it is written, “said unto them, “They need not depart; give ye them to eat.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: He said not, “I give them,” but, “Give ye them;” for as yet their regard to Him was as to a man.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But they not even so are awakened, but still reason as with a man, saying,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “We have but five loaves, and two fishes.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore Mark also saith, “They understood not the saying, for their heart was hardened.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: They continuing therefore to crawl on the ground, then at length He brings in His own part, and saith, “Bring them hither to me.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For although the place be desert, yet He that feeds the world is here; and although the time be now past, yet He that is not subject to time is discoursing with you.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But John saith also, that they were “barley loaves,”not mentioning it without object, but teaching us to trample under foot the pride of costly living.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Such was the diet of the prophets also.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “He took therefore the five loaves, and the two fishes, and commanded the multitude,” it is said, “to sit down upon the grass, and looking up to Heaven, He blessed, and brake, and gave to His disciples, and the disciples to the multitude.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And they did all eat and were filled, and they took up of the fragments that remained twelve baskets full.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And they that had eaten were about five thousand men, beside women and children.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore did He look up to Heaven, and bless?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: It was to be believed of Him, both that He is of the Father, and that He is equal to Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But the proofs of these things seemed to oppose one another.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For while His equality was indicated by His doing all with authority, of His origin from the Father they could no otherwise be persuaded, than by His doing all with great lowliness, and with reference to Him, and invoking Him on His works.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore we see that He neither did these actions only, nor those, that both might be confirmed; and now He works miracles with authority, now with prayer.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Then again, that what He did might not seem an inconsistency, in the lesser things He looks up to Heaven, but in the greater doth all with authority; to teach thee in the lesser also, that not as receiving power from elsewhere, but as honoring Him that begat Him, so He acts.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For example: when He forgave sins, and opened paradise, and brought in the thief, and most utterly set aside the old law, and raised innumerable dead, and bridled the sea, and reproved the un-uttered thoughts of men, and created an eye;—which are achievements of God only and of none else;—we see Him in no instance praying: but when He provided for the loaves to multiply themselves, a far less thing than all these, then He looks up to Heaven; at once establishing these truths which I have spoken of, and instructing us not to touch a meal, until we have given thanks to Him who giveth us this food.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And why doth He not make it of things that are not?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Stopping the mouth of Marcion, and of Manichæans, who alienate His creation from Him, and teaching by His very works, that even all the things that are seen are His works and creatures, and signifying that it is Himself who gives the fruits, who said at the beginning, “Let the earth put forth the herb of grass,” and “Let the waters bring forth things moving with living souls.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For this is not at all a less work than the other.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For though those were made of things that are not, yet nevertheless were they of water; and it was no greater thing to produce fruits out of the earth, and moving things with life out of the water, than out of five loaves to make so many; and of fishes again, which was a sign that He was ruler both of the earth and of the sea.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Thus, since the sick were constantly the subject of His miracles, He works also a general benefit, that the many might not be spectators only of what befell others, but themselves also partakers of the gift.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And that which in the wilderness seemed to the Jews marvellous, (they said at least, “Can He give bread also? or prepare a table in the wilderness?”) this He shows forth in His works.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: With this view also He leads them into the wilderness, that the miracle might be very far beyond suspicion, and that no one might think that any village lying near contributed ought to the meal.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For this reason He mentions the hour also, not the place only.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And another thing too we learn, the self-restraint of the disciples which they practised in necessary things, and how little they accounted of food.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For being twelve, they had five loaves only and two fishes; so secondary to them were the things of the body: so did they cling to the things spiritual only.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And not even that little did they hold fast, but gave up even it when asked.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Whereby we should be taught, that though we have but little, this too we ought to give up to them that are in need.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Thus, when commanded to bring the five loaves, they say not, “and whence are we to have food? whence to appease our own hunger?” but they obey at once.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And besides what I have mentioned, to this end, as I at least think, He makes it out of the materials which they had, namely, that He might lead them to faith; for as yet they were rather in a weak state.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore also “He looks up to Heaven.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For of the other miracles they had many examples, but of this none.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “He took the loaves,” therefore, “and brake them, and gave them by His disciples,” hereby to honor them; and not in honor to them only, but also that, when the miracle had been done they might not disbelieve it, nor forget it when it had past, their own hands bearing them witness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore also He suffers the multitudes first to have a sense of hunger, and waits for these to come to Him first and ask Him, and by them makes the people sit down, and by them distributes; being minded by their own confessions and actions to prepossess them every one.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Therefore also, from them He receives the loaves, that the testimonies of what was doing might be many, and that they might have memorials of the miracle.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For if even after these occurrences they forgot,what would not have been their case, had He omitted those provisions?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And He commands them to sit down on the trampled grass, instructing the multitudes in self-denial.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For His will was not to feed their bodies only, but also to instruct their souls.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: As well by the place therefore, as by His giving them nothing more than loaves and fishes, and by setting the same before all, and making it common, and by affording no one more than another, He was teaching them humility, and temperance, and charity, and to be of like mind one towards another, and to account all things common.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “And He brake and gave to the disciples, and the disciples to the multitude.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: The five loaves He brake and gave, and the five multiplied themselves in the hands of the disciples.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And not even here doth He stay the miracle, but He made them even to exceed; to exceed, not as whole loaves, but as fragments; to signify that of those loaves these were remains, and in order that the absent might learn what had been done.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For this purpose indeed He suffered the multitudes to hunger, that no one might suppose what took place to be illusion.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For this also He caused just twelve baskets to remain over, that Judas also might bear one.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For He was able indeed to have appeased their hunger, but the disciples would not have known His power, since in Elijah’s case also this took place.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: At all events, so greatly were the Jews amazed at Him for this, that they wished even to make Him a king,although with regard to the other miracles they did not so in any instance.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What reasoning now may set forth, how the loaves multipliedthemselves; how they flowed together in the wilderness; how they were enough for so many (for there were “five thousand men beside women and children;” which was a very great commendation of the people, that both women and men attended Him); how the remnants had their being (for this again is not less than the former), and became so abundant, that the baskets were equal in number to the disciples, and neither more nor less?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Having then taken the fragments, He gave them not to the multitudes, but to the disciples, and that, because the multitudes were in a more imperfect state than the disciples.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And, having wrought the miracle, “straightway He constrained His disciples to get into a ship, and to go before Him unto the other side, while He sent the multitudes away.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For even if He had seemed, when in sight, to be presenting an illusion, and not to have wrought a truth; yet surely not in His absence also.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For this cause then, submitting His proceedings to an exact test, He commanded those that had got the memorials, and the proof of the miracles, to depart from Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And besides this, when He is doing great works, He disposes elsewhere of the multitudes and the disciples, instructing us in nothing to follow after the glory that comes from the people, nor to collect a crowd about us.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Now by saying, “He constrained them,” He indicates the very close attendance of the disciples.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And His pretext indeed for dismissing them was the multitude, but He was Himself minded to go up into the mountain; and He did this, instructing us neither to be always in intercourse with multitudes, nor always to fly from the crowd, but each of the two as may be expedient, and giving each duly his turn.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Let us learn therefore ourselves also to wait upon Jesus; but not for His bounty in things sensible, lest we be upbraided like the Jews.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For “ye seek me,” saith He, “not because ye saw the miracles,but because ye did eat of the loaves, and were filled.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Therefore neither doth He work this miracle continually, but a second time only; that they might be taught not to be slaves to their belly, but to cling incessantly to the things of the Spirit.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: To these then let us also cling, and let us seek the heavenly bread, and having received it, let us cast away all worldly care.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For if those men left houses, and cities, and kinsmen, and all, and abode in the wilderness, and when hunger was pressing, withdrew not; much more ought we, when approaching such a table, to show forth a more abundant self-command, and to set our love on the things of the Spirit, and to seek the things of sense as secondary to these.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Since even they were blamed, not because they sought Him for the bread, but because it was for this only they sought Him, and for this primarily.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For should any one despise the great gifts, but cling to the small, and to those which the giver would have him despise, he loses these latter too: as on the other hand, if we love those, He adds these also.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For these are but an appendage to the others; so vile are they and trifling, compared with those, although they be great.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Let us not therefore spend our diligence on them, but account both the acquisition and loss of them alike indifferent, even as Job also neither clung to them when present, nor sought them absent.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For on this account, they are called χρματα,not that we should bury them in the earth, but that we should use them aright.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And as of artisans every one hath his peculiar skill, even so the rich man, as he knows not how to work in brass, nor to frame ships, nor to weave, nor to build houses, nor any such thing;—let him learn then to use his wealth aright, and to pity the poor; so shall he know a better art than all those.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For indeed this is above all those arts.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Its workshop is builded in Heaven.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: It hath its tools not of iron and brass, but of goodness and of a right will.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Of this art Christ is the Teacher, and His Father.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “For be ye merciful,” saith He, “as your Father which is in Heaven.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And what is indeed marvellous, being so much superior to the rest, it needs no labor, no time for its perfection; it is enough to have willed, and the whole is accomplished.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But let us see also the end thereof, what it is.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What then is the end of it?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Heaven, the good things in the heavens, that unspeakable glory, the spiritual bride-chambers, the bright lamps, the abiding with the Bridegroom; the other things, which no speech, nor even understanding, is able to set forth.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: So that herein likewise great is its difference from all others.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For most of the arts profit us for the present life, but this for the life to come also.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But if it so far excels the arts that are necessary to us for the present, as medicine, for instance, and house-building, and all others like them: much more the rest, which if any one were nicely to examine, he would not even allow them to be arts.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore I at least would not call those others, as they are unnecessary, so much as arts at all.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For wherein is delicate cookery and making sauces profitable to us?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Nowhere: yea, they are greatly unprofitable and hurtful, doing harm both to body and soul, by bringing upon us the parent of all diseases and sufferings, luxury, together with great extravagance.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But not these only, but not even painting, or embroidery, would I for one allow to be an art, for they do but throw men into useless expense.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But the arts ought to be concerned with things necessary and important to our life, to supply and work them up.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For to this end God gave us skill at all, that we might invent methods, whereby to furnish out our life.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But that there should be figureseither on walls, or on garments, wherein is it useful, I pray thee?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For this same cause the sandal-makers too, and the weavers, should have great retrenchments made in their art.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For most things in it they have carried into vulgar ostentation,having corrupted its necessary use, and mixed with an honest art an evil craft; which has been the case with the art of building also.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But even as to this, so long as it builds houses and not theatres, and labors upon things necessary, and not superfluous, I give the name of an art; so the business of weaving too, as long as it makes clothes, and coverlids, but does not imitate the spiders, and overwhelm men with much absurdity, and unspeakable effeminacy, so long I call it an art.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And the sandal-makers’ trade, so long as it makes sandals, I will not rob of the appellation of art; but when it perverts men to the gestures of women, and causes them by their sandals to grow wanton and delicate, we will set it amidst the things hurtful and superfluous, and not so much as name it an art.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And I know well, that to many I seem over-minute in busying myself about these things; I shall not however refrain for this.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For the cause of all our evils is this, such faults being at all counted trifling, and therefore disregarded.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And what sin, say you, can be of less account than this, of having an ornamented and glittering sandal, which fits the foot; if indeed it seem right at all to denominate it a sin?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Will ye then that I let loose my tongue upon it, and show its unseemliness, how great it is? and will ye not be angry?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Or rather, though ye be angry, I care not much.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Nay, for yourselves are to blame for this folly, who do not so much as think it is a sin, and hereby constrain us to enter upon the reproof of this extravagance.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Come then, let us examine it, and let us see what sort of an evil it is.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For when the silken threads, which it is not seemly should be even inwoven in your garments, these are sewn by you into your shoes, what reproach, what derision do these things deserve?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And if thou despise our judgments, hear the voice of Paul, with great earnestness forbidding these things, and then thou wilt perceive the absurdity of them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What then saith he?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Not with braided hair, or gold, or pearls, or costly array.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Of what favor then canst thou be worthy; when, in spite of Paul’s prohibiting the married woman to have costly clothing, thou extendest this effeminacy even to thy shoes, and hast no end of contri vances for the sake of this ridicule and reproach?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Yes: for first a ship is built, then rowers are mustered, and a man for the prow, and a helmsman, and a sail is spread, and an ocean traversed, and, leaving wife and children and country, the merchant commits his very life to the waves, and comes to the land of the barbarians, and undergoes innumerable dangers for these threads, that after it all thou mayest take them, and sew them into thy shoes, and ornament the leather.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And what can be done worse than this folly?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But the old ways are not like these, but such as become men.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore I for my part expect that in process of time the young men amongst us will wear even women’s shoes, and not be ashamed.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And what is more grievous, men’s fathers seeing these things are not much displeased, but do even account it an indifferent matter.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Would ye that I should add what is still more grievous; that these things are done even when there are many poor?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Would ye that I bring before you Christ, an hungered, naked, wandering everywhere, in chains?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And how many thunderbolts must ye not deserve, overlooking Him in want of necessary food, and adorning these pieces of leather with so much diligence?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And He indeed, when He was giving law to His disciples, would not so much as suffer them to have shoes at all, but we cannot bear to walk, I say not barefooted, but even with feet shod as they ought to be.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What then can be worse than this unseemliness, this absurdity?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For the thing marks a soul, in the first place effeminate, then unfeeling and cruel, then curious and idly busy.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For when will he be able to attend to any necessary matter, who is taken up with these superfluous things? when will such a youth endure to take heed to his soul, or to consider so much as that he hath a soul?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Yes, he surely will be a trifler who cannot help admiring such things; he cruel, who for their sake neglects the poor; he void of virtue, who spends all his diligence on them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For he that is curious about the beauty of threads, and the bloom of colors, and the tendrils made of such woven work, when will he be able to look upon the heaven? when will he admire the beauty there, who is excited about a kind of beauty that belongs to pieces of leather, and who is bending to the earth?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And whereas God hath stretched out the Heaven, and lighted up the sun, drawing thy looks upwards; thou constrainest thyself to look downwards, and to the earth, like the swine, and obeyest the devil.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For indeed this wicked demon hath devised this unseemliness, to draw thee off from that beauty.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For this intent hath he drawn thee this way; and God, showing Heaven, is outvied by a devil showing certain skins, or rather not even skins (for indeed these too are God’s works), but effeminacy and a bad kind of skill.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And the young man goes about bending down towards the earth, he that is required to seek wisdom concerning the things in Heaven; priding himself more on these trifles than if he had accomplished some great and good work, and walking on tiptoe in the forum, and hereby begetting to himself superfluous sorrows and distresses, lest he should stain them with the mud when it is winter; lest he should cover them with the dust, when summer is come.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What sayest thou, O man?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Hast thou cast thy whole soul into the mire through this extravagance, and dost thou overlook it trailing on the ground, and art thou so anxious about a pair of shoes?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Mark their use, and respect the verdict thou passest on them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For to tread on mud and mire, and all the spots on the pavement, for this were thy shoes made.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Or if thou canst not bear this, take and hang them from thy neck, or put them on thy head.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And ye indeed laugh at hearing this.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But I am inclined to weep for these men’s madness, and their earnest care about these matters.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For in truth they would rather stain their body with mud, than those pieces of leather.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Triflers then they become in this way, and fond of money again in another way.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For he that has been used to be frantic and eager upon such matters, requires also for his clothes and for all other things much expense, and a large income.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And if he have a munificent father, his thraldom becomes worse, his absurd fancy more intense; but if a parsimonious one, he is driven to other unseemliness, by way of getting together a little money for such expenses.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Hence many young men have even sold their manhood, and have become parasites to the rich, and have undertaken other servile offices, purchasing thereby the fulfillment of such desires.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: So then, that this man is sure to be at once fond of money, and a trifler, and about important things the most indolent of all men, and that he will be forced to commit many sins, is hereby evident.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And that he is cruel and vainglorious, neither this will any one gainsay: cruel, in that when he sees a poor man, through the love of finery he makes as though he did not even see him, but while he is decking out these things with gold, overlooks him perishing of hunger; vainglorious, since even in such little matters he trains himself to hunt after the admiration of the beholders.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For I suppose no general prides himself so much on his legions and trophies, as our profligate youths on the decking out of their shoes, on their trailing garments, on the dressing of their hair; yet surely all these are works of other persons, in their trades.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But if men do not cease from vain boasting in the works of others, when will they cease from it in their own?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Shall I mention yet other things more grievous than these? or are even these enough for you?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Well then; I must end my speech here; since even this have I said, because of the disputatious, who maintain the thing not to be so very wrong.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And although I know that many of the young will not so much as attend to what I have said, being once for all intoxicated with this fancy, I yet ought not therefore to keep silence.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For such fathers as have understanding, and are as yet sound, will be able to force them, even against their will, to a becoming decency.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Say not then, “this is of no consequence, that is of no consequence;” for this, this hath ruined all.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For even hereby ought you to train them, and by the things which seem trifling to make them grave, great of soul, superior to outward habiliments; so shall we find them approved in the great things also.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For what is more ordinary than the learning of letters? nevertheless thereby do men become rhetoricians,and sophists, and philosophers, and if they know not their letters, neither will they ever have that knowledge.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And this we have spoken not to young men only, but to women also, and to young damsels.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For these too are liable to the like charges, and much more, inasmuch as seemliness is a thing appropriate to a virgin.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What has been said therefore to the others; do ye account to have been said to you also, that we may not repeat again the same things.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For it is full time now to close our discourse with prayer.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: All of you then pray with us, that the young men of the church above all things may be enabled to live orderly, and to attain an old age becoming them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Since for those surely who do not so live, it were well not to come to old age at all.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But for them that have grown old even in youth, I pray that they may attain also to the very deep of gray hairs, and become fathers of approved children, and may be a joy to them that gave them birth, and above all surely to the God that made them, and may exterminate every distempered fancy, not that about their shoes, nor about their clothes only, but every other kind also.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For as untilled land, such is also youth neglected, bringing forth many thorns from many quarters.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Let us then send forth on them the fire of the Spirit, and burn up these wicked desires, and let us break up our fields, and make them ready for the reception of the seed, and the young men amongst us let us exhibit with soberer minds than the old elsewhere.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For this in fact is the marvellous thing, when temperance shines forth in youth; since he surely that is temperate in old age cannot have a great reward, having in perfection the security from his age.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But what is wonderful, is to enjoy a calm amidst waves, and in a furnace not to be burnt, and in youth not to run wanton.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: With these things then in our minds, let us emulate that blessed Joseph, who shone through all these trials, that we may attain unto the same crowns with him; unto which may we all attain, by the grace and love towards man of our Lord Jesus Christ, with whom be glory unto the Father, together with the Holy Ghost, now and always, and world without end.
